behaviors.behavior: In authoritative mode the honest-ceiling note is the authoritative one — it no longer falsely claims to be a 'heuristic estimate from the outside' and states it measures control discipline, not behaviour-correctness
behaviors.evidence: test_report_model.py::TestNaSemantics::test_authoritative_ceiling_note_drops_the_heuristic_claim + empirically verified on the shipwright repo (--allow-network → authoritative A/99.9, corrected footer)

behaviors.behavior: In heuristic (cold-repo) mode the honest-ceiling note keeps the outside-estimate wording
The change-reconciliation provenance source no longer leaks the internal 'BP-2' codename (source stays jargon-free)
The whole HTML report is codename-free (no 'BP-2' anywhere), including the reconciliation card's expandable provenance
The mode-appropriate note flows through the view-model to every renderer; HTML golden regenerated & byte-exact; ruff clean
behaviors.evidence: test_render_snapshots.py + ruff@0.15.15 (report_model.py the only src change)
